assertion_results.text: The response identifies that the Implementation Notes section is missing from the task description and flags this as a gap requiring clarification (constraint 1.6)
assertion_results.evidence: The table on line 27 marks 'Implementation Notes | **MISSING**'. Section '2. Missing: Implementation Notes section' (lines 46-51) expands with six specific bullets detailing what is absent. The response halts execution citing this gap per constraint 1.6 (lines 73-79).

assertion_results.text: The response identifies that the Acceptance Criteria are vague — specifically calling out 'search results are relevant' and 'performance is acceptable' as insufficiently specific for implementation
assertion_results.evidence: Section '3. Vague Acceptance Criteria' (lines 53-59) explicitly quotes both phrases: '"Search results are relevant" -- what defines relevance? Exact match? Substring? Ranked results?' and '"Performance is acceptable" -- what are the performance targets? Response time threshold? Maximum result set size?' Both are called out as not measurable.

assertion_results.text: The response identifies that the Test Requirements are vague — 'test that search works correctly' does not define what 'correctly' means
assertion_results.evidence: Section '4. Vague Test Requirements' (lines 61-70) states: 'The single test requirement ("Test that search works correctly") provides no concrete test cases.' It then lists five specific test scenarios that should have been specified but were not, effectively identifying that 'correctly' is undefined.

assertion_results.text: The response asks the user for clarification rather than improvising implementation details — it does not proceed to write a full implementation plan (constraint 1.6)
assertion_results.evidence: The title states 'Execution Halted (Incomplete Description)'. Lines 77-79 explicitly state: 'No implementation plan has been drafted. No branches have been created. No code changes have been made.' The 'Clarifying Questions' section (lines 83-97) asks 6 questions and requests the user provide missing information before proceeding.

assertion_results.text: The response asks at least 3 specific clarifying questions about: which fields to search, what relevance ranking means, and what performance threshold is required
assertion_results.evidence: The Clarifying Questions section contains 6 questions. (1) Which fields to search: Q3 (line 89) asks 'Which fields of a package are searchable (name, version, license, PURL, description)?'. (2) Relevance ranking: The gaps section (line 58) asks 'what defines relevance? Exact match? Substring? Ranked results?' and Q2 (line 87) asks about search mechanism/strategy. (3) Performance threshold: The gaps section (line 59) asks 'what are the performance targets? Response time threshold?' and Q5 (line 93) asks for 'concrete, measurable criteria' with an example including '500ms for datasets up to 10,000 packages'. All three topics are covered across the clarifying questions and identified gaps.
